title: FASTP Quality Control Metrics Per Group
description: Per-group quality control metrics from FASTP, as produced by the DOWNSTREAM workflow. The top-level object is keyed by sample name; each entry contains the raw FASTP JSON output with injected 'sample' and 'group' fields. Produced for Illumina/short-read platforms only (ONT uses FILTLONG instead).

sample_entry.description: FASTP output for a single sample, with pipeline-injected metadata. The 'sample', 'group', 'summary', and 'filtering_result' fields are always present; the remaining fields depend on sequencing mode (single-end vs paired-end) and FASTP flags.

sample.description: Sample identifier, injected by the pipeline.

group.description: Group identifier, injected by the pipeline.

summary.description: High-level aggregate statistics (total reads, bases, quality rates) before and after filtering.

filtering_result.description: Breakdown of how many reads were removed by each filter category.

adapter_cutting.description: Adapter trimming statistics. Present when adapter trimming is enabled (--adapter_fasta or --detect_adapter_for_pe).

duplication.description: PCR duplication rate estimate. Only present when duplication evaluation is enabled (absent with --dont_eval_duplication, which this pipeline uses).

insert_size.description: Fragment length distribution. Only present for paired-end data.

polyx_trimming.description: Poly-X tail trimming statistics. Only present when --trim_poly_x is used (this pipeline: always).

read1_before_filtering.description: Per-base and per-read quality statistics for read 1 before QC filtering.

read1_after_filtering.description: Per-base and per-read quality statistics for read 1 after QC filtering.

read2_before_filtering.description: Per-base and per-read quality statistics for read 2 before QC filtering. Only present for paired-end data.

read2_after_filtering.description: Per-base and per-read quality statistics for read 2 after QC filtering. Only present for paired-end data.

merged_and_filtered.description: Per-base and per-read quality statistics for merged reads after filtering. Only present when --merge is used (replaces read1_after_filtering).

command.description: The full FASTP command line that was executed, including all flags and arguments.

summary.description: Overall statistics before and after filtering.

fastp_version.description: Version of fastp that produced this output.

sequencing.description: Sequencing mode description, e.g. 'paired end (151 cycles + 151 cycles)' or 'single end (151 cycles)'.

before_filtering.description: Aggregate read and quality statistics before any QC filtering.

after_filtering.description: Aggregate read and quality statistics after QC filtering.

summary_stats.description: Aggregate read statistics for a filtering stage.

q20_bases.description: Number of bases with quality >= Q20.

q30_bases.description: Number of bases with quality >= Q30.

q20_rate.description: Fraction of bases with quality >= Q20.

q30_rate.description: Fraction of bases with quality >= Q30.

read2_mean_length.description: Mean length of read 2. Only present for paired-end data.

filtering_result.description: Breakdown of reads removed during QC filtering.

passed_filter_reads.description: Number of reads that passed all filters.

corrected_reads.description: Number of reads corrected by overlap analysis. Only present when --correction is used (this pipeline: always).

corrected_bases.description: Number of bases corrected by overlap analysis. Only present when --correction is used (this pipeline: always).

low_quality_reads.description: Number of reads removed due to low quality.

too_many_N_reads.description: Number of reads removed due to excessive N bases.

low_complexity_reads.description: Number of reads removed due to low complexity. Only present when --low_complexity_filter is used (this pipeline: always).

too_short_reads.description: Number of reads removed due to being too short.

too_long_reads.description: Number of reads removed due to being too long.

adapter_trimmed_reads.description: Number of reads with adapters trimmed.

adapter_trimmed_bases.description: Total number of adapter bases trimmed.

read2_adapter_sequence.description: Detected adapter sequence for read 2. Only present for paired-end data.

read1_adapter_counts.description: Adapter match frequency by overlap length for read 1.

read2_adapter_counts.description: Adapter match frequency by overlap length for read 2. Only present for paired-end data.

duplication.description: PCR duplication analysis results. Only present when duplication evaluation is enabled (absent with --dont_eval_duplication). Contains only 'rate' — fastp does not write a histogram to JSON.

insert_size.description: Fragment length distribution for paired-end data. Not present for single-end data.

unknown.description: Number of read pairs with unknown insert size.

polyx_trimming.description: Poly-X tail trimming statistics, broken down by base (A, T, C, G).

total_polyx_trimmed_reads.description: Total number of reads with poly-X tails trimmed.

polyx_trimmed_reads.description: Number of reads trimmed per base type.

total_polyx_trimmed_bases.description: Total number of bases trimmed from poly-X tails.

polyx_trimmed_bases.description: Number of bases trimmed per base type.

read_quality_stats.description: Per-read quality and base composition statistics.

q20_bases.description: Number of bases with quality >= Q20.

q30_bases.description: Number of bases with quality >= Q30.

total_cycles.description: Number of sequencing cycles (i.e. read length before trimming).

quality_curves.description: Per-cycle quality score curves (e.g. 'mean' quality at each sequencing cycle).

content_curves.description: Per-cycle base composition curves (e.g. fraction of A, T, C, G, N at each cycle).

kmer_count.description: K-mer frequency counts, keyed by k-mer sequence string.

overrepresented_sequences.description: Overrepresented sequences and their counts. May be empty if no sequences exceed the reporting threshold.

per_cycle_curves.description: Per-cycle quality or base composition curves, keyed by metric name (e.g. 'A', 'T', 'C', 'G', 'mean', 'N', 'GC').
